---
title: "National Trends" 
format:
  html:
    css: styles.css
    embed-resources: TRUE
    code-fold: true
    page-layout: full
    fig_caption: yes
    toc: false
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  dev = "ragg_png"
)

source("prep/library.R")
source("prep/functions.R")
source("prep/dataframes.R")
```

```{r}
page_summary <- "With the support of Arnold Ventures, the Council of State Governments (CSG) Justice Center analyzed data from the National Corrections Reporting Program (NCRP), Bureau of Justice Statistics (BJS) Prisoner Series, and Robina Institute of Criminal Law and Criminal Justice to gain deeper insights into the relationship between parole practices and prison populations. The study specifically focused on understanding the dynamics of parole eligibility and lengths of stay, with a specific emphasis on examining racial, ethnic, and gender disparities."
  
num_people_current   <- sum(parole_eligibility_table_select_year$current_count, na.rm = TRUE)
num_people_1_5_years <- sum(parole_eligibility_table_select_year$future_1_5_years_count, na.rm = TRUE)
```

::: narrow-section
```{r}
div(id = "body-section",
    div(id = "regular-text", page_summary, br(), br(), br())
    )

# # combine info in one row and center
# # highlighted national trends numbers
htmltools::div(
  style = "display:flex; flex-direction: row; justify-content: center; align-items: center;",
  div(
    style = "text-align: center; margin-right: 10em;",
    div(
      id = "highlight-text", 
      formattable::comma(num_people_current, digits = 0)
    ),
    div(
      HTML("Currently Eligible for Parole<br>but in Prison"),
      id = "bold-text"
    )
  ),
  div(
    style = "text-align: center;",
    div(
      id = "highlight-text", 
      formattable::comma(num_people_1_5_years, digits = 0)
    ),
    div(
      HTML("Eligible for Parole in the Next 1-5 Years<br>but in Prison"),
      id = "bold-text"
    )
  )
)
```
:::

<br><br>

```{r}
div(id = "body-section", br(),
    div(id = "big-section-title", "People in Prison Currently Eligible for Parole"), br(),
    div(id = "regular-text", "These maps show the percentage and number of individuals in prison in each state during 2020 who, although eligible for parole, remained incarcerated. Toggle between viewing data based on the percentage of these individuals or the actual number of eligible people still in prison. The stark variations across states emphasize the diversity of parole policies and practices in the U.S."), br()
    )
```

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Percentage of People
```{r}
map_percent %>% 
  hc_size(height = 550) %>% 
  hc_title(text = paste0("The Percentage of People who were Eligible for Parole but still in Prison in ", select_year)) %>% 
    hc_exporting(enabled = TRUE,
               buttons = list(contextButton = list(align = 'right',
                                                   verticalAlign = 'top',
                                                   y = 0,
                                                   x = -50))) 
```

## Number of People
```{r}
map_count %>% 
  hc_size(height = 550) %>% 
    hc_title(text = paste0("The Number of People who were Eligible for Parole but still in Prison in ", select_year)) %>% 
    hc_exporting(enabled = TRUE,
               buttons = list(contextButton = list(align = 'right',
                                                   verticalAlign = 'top',
                                                   y = 0,
                                                   x = 0))) 
```

:::

```{r}
div(id = "body-section", br(),
    div(id = "note-text", "Notes: NCRP Data is from 2020. The parole-eligible prison population only includes people with sentence lengths ranging between 1-25 years and people incarcerated for a new crime."), br()
    )
```

```{r}
div(id = "body-section", br(),
    div(id = "big-section-title", "Other National Visualization Ideas"), br(),
    div(id = "regular-text", "People Infographics: National disparities for race, ethnicity, and gender"), br(),
    div(id = "regular-text", "Stacked Bar Chart: National proportion of the parole-eligible population that was released each year compared to those who remained incarcerated despite their parole eligibility (2010-2020)"), br(),
    div(id = "regular-text", "Highlighted Finding: Depict the average length of time people are incarcerated after their parole eligibility date."), br(),
    div(id = "regular-text", "Stacked Bar Chart: Parole eligibility by offense type. Bars representing different crime categories (e.g., violent crimes, property crimes) and their corresponding parole-eligible numbers."), br(),
    div(id = "regular-text", "List: 'Top States' in any of the findings above."), br(),
    div(id = "regular-text", "Map: Robina Institute's prison sentencing system rating."), br(),
    div(id = "regular-text", "Table: Simpler table than the one below. Parole-eligible prison populations by state"), br()

    )
```


<br>

```{r}
div(id = "body-section", br(),
    div(id = "big-section-title", "Parole Eligibility Data by State"), br(),
    div(id = "regular-text", "
The table provides a comprehensive overview of state-by-state data on the number of people in prison and their parole eligibility status, including the percentage of individuals currently eligible for parole, those who will be eligible in the next 1-5 years, and the proportion for which parole eligibility data is missing or low, due to the abolition of parole or lack of data in certain states."),
br())
```

::: table-container
```{r}
# Get parole eligibility information
parole_info_by_state_clean <- parole_info_by_state %>%
  select(state, abolished_discretionary_parole)

# Prepare the table data
table_data <- parole_eligibility_table_select_year %>%
  left_join(parole_info_by_state_clean, by = "state") %>% 
  select(
    state,
    abolished_discretionary_parole,
    yearendpop,
    yearendpop_125years_new_crime,
    current_count, 
    current_perc,
    future_1_5_years_count, 
    future_1_5_years_perc, 
    missing_count, 
    missing_perc
  )

# Define common column styles
common_col_style <- list(
  position = "sticky",
  borderRight = "1px solid #d3d3d3"
)

# Create reactable
reactable(
  table_data,
  style = reactable_style,
  theme = reactable_theme,
  
  # Default Column Definitions
  defaultColDef = colDef(
    format = colFormat(separators = TRUE),
    align = "right",
    minWidth = 90
  ),
  
  # Table options
  compact = TRUE,
  fullWidth = FALSE,
  defaultPageSize = 50,
  
  # Column Grouping
  columnGroups = list(
    colGroup(
      name = "Currently Eligible for Parole",
      columns = c("current_perc", "current_count")
    ),
    colGroup(
      name = "Eligible for Parole in the Next 1-5 Years",
      columns = c("future_1_5_years_perc", "future_1_5_years_count")
    ),
    colGroup(
      name = "Missing Parole Eligibility Data",
      columns = c("missing_perc", "missing_count")
    )
  ),
  
  # Individual Column Definitions
  columns = list(
    state = colDef(
      name = "State",
      minWidth = 120,
      align = "left",
      style = list(fontWeight = "bold")
    ),
    abolished_discretionary_parole = colDef(
      name = "Abolished Parole Release",
      minWidth = 100
    ),
    yearendpop = colDef(
      name = "Total Prison Population",
      minWidth = 120,
      headerStyle = list(fontWeight = "bold")
    ),
    yearendpop_125years_new_crime = colDef(
      name = "Total Prison Population (1-25 Year Sentences)",
      minWidth = 140,
      style = common_col_style,
      headerStyle = list(fontWeight = "bold")
    ),
    current_perc = colDef(
      name = "Percent of Prison Population",
      format = colFormat(percent = TRUE, digits = 1),
      style = common_col_style,
      headerStyle = list(fontWeight = "normal")
    ),
    future_1_5_years_perc = colDef(
      name = "Percent of Prison Population",
      format = colFormat(percent = TRUE, digits = 1),
      style = common_col_style,
      headerStyle = list(fontWeight = "normal")
    ),
    missing_perc = colDef(
      name = "Percent of Prison Population",
      format = colFormat(percent = TRUE, digits = 1),
      headerStyle = list(fontWeight = "normal")
    ),
    current_count = colDef(
      name = "Number of People",
      headerStyle = list(fontWeight = "normal")
    ),
    future_1_5_years_count = colDef(
      name = "Number of People",
      headerStyle = list(fontWeight = "normal")
    ),
    missing_count = colDef(
      name = "Number of People",
      headerStyle = list(fontWeight = "normal")
    )
  )
)
```

:::
